payload.answer: The recall system matched both mem_new and mem_old to Sam from Goldman, then applied conflict handling. Because mem_old is superseded by mem_new, mem_old was filtered from the final recall set and only mem_new was returned as the current memory. This prevents older, superseded information from being presented as if it were still current.
payload.decision: Recall returned mem_new because it is the active memory for Sam at Goldman, and excluded mem_old because mem_old is marked as superseded by the newer memory.

entity_resolution.reason: Both memories resolve to the same recall target: Sam in the Goldman context. No canonical entity ID was provided in the fixture.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the currently valid memory associated with Sam from Goldman, so it was eligible for recall.
mem_old refers to the same recall target but is not returned as current because it has been superseded.

details: Matched query target to Sam in Goldman context.
Found a newer active memory: mem_new.
Detected mem_old as superseded relative to the same target.
Applied current-state recall policy and excluded superseded memory.

repair_options: Verify that mem_old has an explicit superseded_by link to mem_new.
Check recall filters to confirm superseded memories are excluded from current results.
Inspect ranking/debug traces to ensure mem_new wins after entity match and conflict resolution.
If both should appear, use a history/audit view instead of current-state recall.
